content:
I mean... The cause of the issue might ultimately be a single character but there may be a lot of reasons that you are mislead... Like not having the environment be consistent, accidentally editing artifacts instead of source code, other issues leading you towards dead end paths, etc.

The story I like the most is that I once fixed a multi year issue with a large distributed system by fixing a single for loop. The change ended up being around 5 lines of code changed but it was the result of a lot of added debugging, analysis of multiple systems working together, and unexpected performance behaviors. Plus I had to learn Java at the same time!

In the end, the lifetime of the system was improved long enough for a complete rewrite to be finished. For a while, it was fun to validate the two systems against each other and the new system became a backup for when there was an issue with the old system. It was when we had to use the backup that we felt comfortable switching over for good. And btw, a lot of the hold up for just switching to the new system was related to really weird legacy behaviors that we had to unwind.

Dealing with large legacy systems can be tough. I learned a lot on that project!
